description.en: A Yemenite Jewish flatbread laminated with butter through repeated rolling and coiling, then pan-fried until deeply golden and shatteringly flaky. A weekend breakfast staple in Israel, topped with grated tomato, boiled egg and fiery zhoug.
description.de: Ein jemenitisch-jüdisches Fladenbrot, durch wiederholtes Rollen und Aufwickeln mit Butter laminiert und in der Pfanne gebraten, bis es tief golden und herrlich blättrig ist. In Israel ein Wochenend-Frühstücksklassiker, garniert mit geriebener Tomate, Ei und scharfer Zhoug-Sauce.
description.es: Un pan plano judío yemenita, laminado con mantequilla mediante enrollados y plegados repetidos y frito en sartén hasta quedar dorado y crujiente en capas. Un clásico desayuno de fin de semana en Israel, cubierto con tomate rallado, huevo duro y el picante zhoug.
description.fr: Un pain plat juif yéménite, feuilleté au beurre par des roulages et pliages répétés, puis poêlé jusqu'à devenir doré et merveilleusement croustillant en couches. Un classique du petit-déjeuner du week-end en Israël, garni de tomate râpée, d'œuf dur et de zhoug épicé.

origin.region: Yemen; now central to Israeli cuisine via Yemenite Jewish immigration
origin.note: Brought to Israel by Yemenite Jewish immigrants in the 20th century; now sold frozen in Israeli supermarkets as a breakfast staple.

ingredients.note: for laminating between folds; recipe also allows oil or margarine

yield.shape: thin laminated square or coiled disc, pan-fried

keeping.note: Traditionally rolled, laminated and frozen uncooked in stacks, frozen malawach is a common Israeli grocery staple, then pan-fried straight from frozen. Cooked malawach is best fresh and hot.

summary.en: Combine flour, salt and baking powder, then stir in boiling water to a soft, smooth dough. Rest 1 hour.
Roll a portion paper-thin into a large rectangle, brush generously with butter, roll into a rope, then coil into a snail shape. Rest about an hour.
Roll the coil out again, brush with more butter, roll and coil again; repeat a third time for maximum layers.
Press each rested coil flat into a round or square just before cooking.
Cook in a hot, lightly oiled skillet, covered, turning until both sides are deep golden and the layers have puffed with steam.
Serve warm with grated tomato, hard-boiled egg and zhoug.

source.adaptation: Salt corrected to a standard 12 g (2 tsp) since the source's own stated 30 g figure for 2 teaspoons of salt was inconsistent with standard salt density.
